assumptions: The developer has Docker installed and local port 8080 available.
No Keycloak server, realm, user, or OpenID Connect client exists yet.
Bootstrap administrator credentials will be created by the documented docker run environment variables.

developer_goal: Start Keycloak, create the first realm and user, register the first OpenID Connect client, and authenticate to Keycloak's SPA testing application.

first_success.outcome: The developer signs in to Keycloak's SPA testing application through the locally running Keycloak server as the created realm user.
first_success.observable_signal: https://www.keycloak.org/app/ completes Sign in against the local Keycloak instance with myuser.

route_selection.surface: Official Keycloak getting-started guides through first SPA sign-in
route_selection.rule: Select one deployment getting-started guide only after peer runtimes that reach the same Admin Console and SPA terminal are ranked under the lexicographic policy.

candidates.name: Docker getting started
candidates.reason: Documents a complete path from docker run through realm, user, client, and SPA sign-in, but Keycloak publishes peer OpenJDK, Podman, Kubernetes, and OpenShift guides to the same conceptual first success.

candidates.name: OpenJDK getting started
candidates.reason: Peer local install path with different prerequisites and install actions before the same Admin Console workflow.

candidates.name: Podman getting started
candidates.reason: Peer container-runtime path rather than a ranked subordinate of Docker.

candidates.name: Kubernetes or OpenShift getting started
candidates.reason: Peer cluster deployment paths that change prerequisites and install actions before administrative setup.

required_path.action: Start Keycloak in development mode with bootstrap administrator credentials via docker run.
required_path.observable_result: Keycloak listens on localhost:8080 with the bootstrap admin user available.

required_path.action: Open the Admin Console and log in with the bootstrap administrator credentials.
required_path.observable_result: The developer is authenticated to the Keycloak Admin Console.
myrealm appears as the current realm.

required_path.action: Create user myuser with first and last name.
Set the user password and toggle Temporary Off.

required_fields.label: Set password submit control
required_fields.notes: The guide describes filling the form and Temporary Off but does not name the exact submit control.

required_path.observable_result: myuser can authenticate with a non-temporary password.

required_path.action: Create OpenID Connect client myclient with redirect URI and web origin for the SPA test app.
required_path.observable_result: myclient is saved for the Keycloak SPA testing application.

required_path.action: Open the SPA testing application, keep the default configuration, and Sign in as myuser.
required_path.observable_result: The SPA testing application completes authentication through the local Keycloak server.

prerequisites.description: Docker must be installed and the host must provide sufficient CPU and memory for Keycloak.
Local port 8080 must be usable for the documented docker run mapping.

unavoidable_waits.description: The first docker run may need to pull the Keycloak image before the server starts.

platform_outcomes.description: Keycloak creates the bootstrap admin user from the docker run environment variables.
The Admin Console and Account Console become available on localhost:8080.

excluded.item: Reading Keycloak guides as measured actions
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is evidence, not a product action.

excluded.item: Optional Account Console profile verification before client creation
excluded.reason: The guide presents Account Console login as an optional verification before securing the first application.

excluded.item: Production database, SSL, and admin password hardening
excluded.reason: The guide places these after the SPA testing first success.

sources.title: Docker: Get started with Keycloak on Docker
evidence_supported: SPA testing application Sign in terminal

sources.title: Running Keycloak in a container
evidence_supported: Container image publication and container runtime context

uncertainties.question: Which getting-started deployment guide is the selected shortest path among Docker, OpenJDK, Podman, Kubernetes, and OpenShift?
uncertainties.impact: Peer install surfaces change prerequisites, commands, waits, and action counts before the shared Admin Console and SPA terminal.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Lexicographic comparison of complete required actions for each official getting-started guide to the same SPA sign-in outcome, or vendor ranking of a default first route.

uncertainties.question: Which exact control submits the Set password form after Temporary is toggled Off?
uncertainties.impact: An omitted UI click would change the required action and field count in the user-credential step.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Current Admin Console observation or a guide update that names the submit control.

uncertainties.question: What exact post-login content in the SPA testing application proves success?
uncertainties.impact: The observable signal can be described only as completed Sign in, not a precise label or field.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated screenshot or walkthrough of the SPA testing application after successful myuser authentication.
